seoTitle: How to Transfer Canva Projects to Another Account
metaDescription: See how to move your Canva projects to a different account, covering exporting, sharing, and keeping your designs intact during the transfer.
intro: Switching Canva accounts doesn't have to mean losing your work, but the process isn't always obvious. This guide walks through exporting your Canva projects, sharing designs across accounts, and keeping everything intact along the way. It's especially useful for freelancers, agencies, and teams consolidating or reorganizing accounts.

steps.heading: Export your Canva projects
steps.body: Start by exporting the designs you want to move so you have a copy ready to bring into the new account.

steps.heading: Share designs across accounts
steps.body: Use Canva's sharing options to transfer projects between accounts rather than starting over from scratch.

steps.heading: Maintain project integrity during transfer
steps.body: Check that fonts, images, and layouts carry over correctly so the design still looks right in the new account.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for the transfer
steps.body: Apply a consistent process for future transfers, especially if you regularly move projects between accounts as clients or teams change.

faq.question: Why would I need to transfer Canva projects to another account?
faq.answer: This is common for freelancers and agencies switching accounts, or teams consolidating projects as businesses grow or change hands.

faq.question: Will my design quality be affected when transferring?
faq.answer: Following the export and sharing steps correctly helps maintain your project's integrity so the design looks the same after transfer.

faq.question: Can I transfer multiple projects at once?
faq.answer: The tutorial focuses on the core export and sharing process, which can be repeated for each project you need to move.

faq.question: Is this process suitable for teams as well as individuals?
faq.answer: Yes, it's useful for both individual account switches and team or agency workflows that need to consolidate projects.
